\begin{document}

\title{Domed Monuments Cluster at Babylonian B\={e}ru Harmonics:\\
A Longitude Enrichment Test on the UNESCO World Heritage List}

\author{Seth Tenenbaum\thanks{ORCID: \href{https://orcid.org/0009-0008-5797-2498}{0009-0008-5797-2498}.
Correspondence: seth@fourthtemple.com}\\
Fourth Temple Research, USA}

\maketitle

\begin{abstract}
A one-dimensional angular enrichment test is applied to the UNESCO World
Heritage Cultural and Mixed corpus ($N=\NclusterTotal$) using the 0.1‑b\={e}ru
interval (3\textdegree{}), the finest integer subdivision of the Babylonian
unit (MUL.APIN, c.~1000~BCE). Mount Gerizim (\GerizimLon\textdegree{}E)
serves as anchor on historical grounds (\S\ref{sec:limitations_circularity}).
Sites within $\leq0.002$~b\={e}ru ($\sim$6.7\,km) of a harmonic node are
Tier‑A+. Domed monuments ($N=\NcircTotal$) show significant A+ enrichment
(Bonferroni $p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestTwo$, $k=\BonfK$), and cluster
asymmetry independently reaches significance
($p_{\mathrm{adj}}=\pAdjTestThree$). The anchor is historically motivated;
the pipeline is fully reproducible.
\end{abstract}

\noindent\textbf{Keywords:}
longitude periodicity, spatial statistics, Levant, UNESCO World Heritage,
Babylonian b\={e}ru, ancient metrology, domed architecture, archaeoastronomy,
monument distribution, MUL.APIN

\bigskip
